title: Yamato discussion featuring Anno
wikitext:
[]: Yamato discussion featuring Anno             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon May 17, 2010 6:03 am A discussion between Hideaki Anno, Leiji Matsumoto and Hiroshi Miyagawa about Yamato, art and other topics Contains various tidbits regarding how Yamato inspired Anno as an artist. And i just love this bit: Matsumoto: It's a very powerful thing, a dream inspired by the feminine. It gives men a lust for life. To perservere through many hardships for the sake of a matchless beauty. Anno: Like hearing the voice of Starsha and flying all the way to Iscandar? I'd go for her, but if it was some scruffy guy instead, I wouldn't answer the call no matter how urgent. I wouldn't believe him! (Laughter)

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Mon May 17, 2010 11:13 pm this is a great find. I love Space Battleship Yamato, and it's especially interesting to hear the guy that wrote that badass score talk about musicians as 'mere workmen'--I often harbor similar sentiments when I'm working on my own stuff for the job or class.
